contentBlocks.title: Why convert BMP to JPG?
contentBlocks.html:
BMP (Bitmap) is an uncompressed image format. A single BMP photo can easily exceed 10–50 MB because every pixel is stored without any compression. This makes BMP impractical for sharing, uploading, or storing large numbers of images.

JPG applies lossy compression that reduces file size by 90–98% with minimal visible quality loss. Converting BMP to JPG produces a compact file that is easy to email, upload, or embed in documents.

This tool processes everything locally in your browser. Your BMP files are never uploaded to any server.

items.title: 1. Add BMP files
items.description: Drag your BMP images onto the upload area or click to select them. No limit on files.

items.title: 2. Set quality
items.description: Choose the JPG quality level. Lower value = smaller file, higher = sharper image.

items.title: 3. Download JPG
items.description: Download each file or grab them all at once. Size comparison shown for every file.

contentBlocks.title: When should you convert BMP to JPG?

items.title: Email attachments
items.description: BMP files are far too large for email. Converting to JPG typically shrinks them by 95%+.

items.title: Storage savings
items.description: A folder of BMP screenshots or scans can consume gigabytes. Converting to JPG frees enormous amounts of disk space.

items.title: Web uploads
items.description: Websites and platforms rarely accept BMP. JPG is accepted everywhere.

items.title: Documents
items.description: Insert compact JPG images into documents instead of bloated BMPs.

contentBlocks.title: Frequently asked questions about BMP to JPG conversion

items.question: How much smaller will the JPG be?
items.answer: BMP files are uncompressed, so the reduction is dramatic. A 20 MB BMP typically becomes 0.5–1.5 MB as JPG at 85% — a 95%+ reduction.

items.question: Does conversion lose quality?
items.answer: JPG is lossy, but at 80–85% quality the difference from the original is negligible.

items.question: Can I convert multiple files at once?
Are files uploaded to a server?
items.answer: No. Everything runs locally in your browser.

items.question: What is the maximum file size?
items.answer: Individual files up to 100 MB are supported.
